title: Polestar provides updates on financial guidance for 2025 and 2024
text: Polestar provided updates on financial guidance for 2025 and 2024, reflecting reassessment of market conditions and tariff impacts. Given the uncertainty surrounding international tariffs and government regulations impacting Polestar's business, the company paused its 2025 financial guidance. This pause reflects unprecedented uncertainty around trade policy and regulatory developments that could significantly impact the company's operations and profitability. Polestar's impairment test assumptions in mid-2025 reflected increases in cost of sales resulting from increased tariffs on imported automotive parts for cars assembled in the US, and pressures on pricing of electric vehicles, which significantly impacted profitability. In Q2 2025, the company reported a net loss of $1.027 billion, wider than the $268 million loss in Q2 2024, due to higher cost of sales including a $739 million impairment expense. The exclusion of the Polestar 5 from U.S. and Chinese markets is a direct response to high U.S. tariffs exceeding 100% on China-made cars and broader trade friction. Polestar will concentrate on European markets for the Polestar 5. Mitigation strategies include producing future models like Polestar 4 in South Korea and Polestar 7 in Europe to reduce tariff impacts and qualify for regional incentives.
